title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
limitacoes: Depends on self-evaluation/test-generation quality; long-term memory is capped in experiments; no formal guarantee of improvement; autonomous code execution requires isolation.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on specific LLM capability and generated tests; some benchmarks/tasks are outside software-engineering workflows; memory size and feedback policies are manually configured.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Self-Taught Optimizer (STOP): Recursively Self-Improving Code Generation
limitacoes: Not full recursive self-improvement because LM weights are unchanged; improvements are stochastic and can be non-monotonic; weaker models can degrade; generated code sometimes attempts to bypass sandbox restrictions.
ameacas_validade: Small downstream task set; synthetic algorithmic utilities; results depend on model version and utility design; sandbox detection uses syntactic checks.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Empowering Agile-Based Generative Software Development through Human-AI Teamwork
limitacoes: Human-in-the-loop workflow; memory-pool contribution is embedded in a larger AgileGen system; no isolated memory-only causal estimate in the extracted results.
ameacas_validade: Dataset/task selection, generated tests, user-evaluation subjectivity, and LLM version differences may affect generalization.
custo_vs_baseline: AgileGen GPT3.5 reports $0.0110 average cost, lower than AutoGPT GPT3.5 ($0.0442), ChatDev_23 ($0.0259), and ChatDev_24 ($0.0192), but higher than GPT3.5+CoT ($0.0028).

title: FALCON: Feedback-driven Adaptive Long/short-term memory reinforced Coding OptimizatioN
limitacoes: Preprint-style reporting; no cost data; memory and reward components are evaluated inside a broader fine-tuning/RL setup.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark-specific tuning, base-model differences, and unclear public-code permanence may affect reproducibility; CODAL-Bench preference outcomes are not directly comparable to functional pass rates.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Iterative Experience Refinement of Software-Developing Agents
limitacoes: Avaliação apenas com ChatGPT-3.5; métricas customizadas podem não refletir qualidade real de software; SRDD limitado a geração de software simples; sem avaliação em benchmarks padrão (SWE-bench); duração aumenta com IER (~180s vs ~148s ChatDev)
ameacas_validade: ChatGPT-3.5 como único modelo; Completeness/Executability/Consistency são proxies automatizados sem validação humana; SRDD gera software simples não representativo de projetos reais; embeddings ada-002 podem não capturar similaridade semântica de código adequadamente
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: LLMs as Continuous Learners: Improving the Reproduction of Defective Code in Software Issues
limitacoes: Focuses on issue reproduction rather than full patch generation; actor/reflection LLM model details are not explicit in extracted text; unresolved cases remain due to model limitations and ambiguous issue descriptions.
ameacas_validade: LLM-as-judge and human judging may introduce evaluation subjectivity; SWE-bench Lite subset and reproduction-task framing may not generalize to all issue-resolution workflows.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Skill-Adpative Imitation Learning for UI Test Reuse
limitacoes: Focuses on Android UI test migration; no cost/token data; source-code availability not explicit in extracted text; success depends on LLM and UI-state representations.
ameacas_validade: Dataset composition, time limits for baselines, prompt engineering, and app/UI heterogeneity may affect external validity.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: A Self-Improving Coding Agent
limitacoes: Novel self-modification ideas are hard to generate; poor early ideas can create path dependence; benchmark timeouts may depress initial scores; much improvement comes from faster editing and cost reductions rather than only higher SWE-Bench capability; safety requires observability and evaluation gates.
ameacas_validade: Random benchmark subsets, short timeouts, cost caps, model/provider choice, and the fixed benchmark set affect external validity; no model-weight updates are studied.
custo_vs_baseline: Cost and time are included in the utility, but the paper does not claim a clean cost-reduction result versus a fixed baseline; average per-problem cost at iteration 0 is $1.91 and at iteration 15 is $1.70.

title: AccelOpt: A Self-Improving LLM Agentic System for AI Accelerator Kernel Optimization
limitacoes: Avaliação limitada a kernels NKI para AWS Trainium; LLMs não conseguem otimizar certos kernels próximos ao pico ou com espaço de otimização difícil; dependência de serviço de profiling distribuído; apenas modelos open-source testados como base
ameacas_validade: NKIBench contém apenas 14 kernels; Trainium é acelerador específico, resultados podem não generalizar para GPUs/TPUs; modelos open-source específicos testados; custo medido por preço de API que pode variar
custo_vs_baseline: 26x mais barato que Claude Sonnet 4 com desempenho equivalente ($139 AccelOpt vs ~$1733 Claude Sonnet 4); memória economiza 16-17% do custo vs beam search puro

title: Agent KB: Leveraging Cross-Domain Experience for Agentic Problem Solving
limitacoes: External experiences can cause knowledge interference; the design needs disagreement gating, conflict resolution, and adaptive eviction; results depend on source-experience quality and stronger-to-weaker transfer.
ameacas_validade: Cross-framework compatibility, source benchmark selection, generated-experience abstraction quality, model mix, and budget caps may affect transferability.
custo_vs_baseline: AGENT KB retrieval overhead is reported as negligible: less than 0.4% of GAIA run cost and average SWE-bench hinting cost below $0.004 per issue.

title: AlphaOPT: Formulating Optimization Programs with Self-Improving LLM Experience Library
limitacoes: Focused on optimization modeling and solver-backed code; relies on solver verification and task ground-truth objectives; no token or monetary cost reporting; theoretical convergence argument is presented as a principled sketch rather than a strict theorem.
ameacas_validade: Dataset overlap in competing fine-tuning baselines, stochasticity in LLM outputs, task-family distribution, and solver-specific verification rules may affect external validity.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Autonomous Code Evolution Meets NP-Completeness
limitacoes: Fully automated operation struggled; the effective setup used targeted human intervention for high-level direction; agents were prone to SAT/UNSAT correctness failures and memory errors such as segmentation faults; domain-specific idea generation remained weak.
ameacas_validade: Hardware/evaluator differences versus official SAT Competition runs, domain-specific SAT benchmarks, human steering in later stages, and repository-scale verification choices affect reproducibility and generalization.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: CodeDistiller: Automatically Generating Code Libraries for Scientific Coding Agents
limitacoes: LLM-as-judge overestimates success, especially for GPT-OSS-120B; unsuccessful examples cost more because debugging reaches hard iteration limits; domain-expert evaluation is limited to a subsample and downstream tasks are materials-science specific.
ameacas_validade: Materials-science domain focus, repository filtering, domain-expert subsampling, judge-model preference evaluation, run budget, and debug-iteration caps may affect external validity.
custo_vs_baseline: Claude-generated examples cost 19 times more on average than GPT-OSS examples; no downstream cost reduction claim is reported.

title: Confucius Code Agent: Scalable Agent Scaffolding for Real-World Codebases
limitacoes: PyTorch-Bench não diretamente comparável com SWE-Bench-Pro por diferenças de ambiente de execução; avaliação de notas limitada a 151 instâncias; meta-agent co-desenvolvido com benchmarks de avaliação; SDK não totalmente open-source
ameacas_validade: SWE-Bench-Verified sensível ao thinking budget do modelo; comparação com sistemas proprietários (Anthropic, OpenAI) pode ter diferenças não controladas de infraestrutura; meta-agent refinado nos mesmos benchmarks de avaliação pode levar a overfitting
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Darwin Godel Machine: Open-Ended Evolution of Self-Improving Agents
limitacoes: Requires extensive compute and significant API cost; assumes coding benchmark gains reflect better self-improvement ability; archive maintenance/parent selection is fixed rather than self-modified; safety requires sandboxing, resource limits, and human oversight.
ameacas_validade: Uses staged subsets for cost control; results depend on Claude/o3-mini models, benchmark sampling, stochastic self-modification, and sandboxed execution constraints.
custo_vs_baseline: DGM SWE-bench run is about $22,000 versus about $10,000 for either baseline run, so performance gains come at materially higher API cost.

title: ECO: An LLM-Driven Efficient Code Optimizer for Warehouse Scale Computers
limitacoes: Operated by human engineers who choose anti-patterns and intervene when needed; all code requires code review; system is evaluated in a proprietary Google production environment; no public source release.
ameacas_validade: Production-internal codebase, sanitized examples, human-review policy, continuous profiler assumptions, anti-pattern selection, and infrastructure-specific normalized-core metric may limit external replication.
custo_vs_baseline: Reports normalized-core resource savings rather than per-task API cost; no direct monetary LLM-cost baseline is provided.

title: EvoLattice: Persistent Internal-Population Evolution through Multi-Alternative Quality-Diversity Graph Representations for LLM-Guided Program Discovery
limitacoes: Combinatorial path growth creates scalability pressure; evaluating all paths becomes costly as nodes and alternatives increase; pruning introduces a tradeoff between exploration and compactness.
ameacas_validade: Training-free NAS proxy benchmark focus, synthetic/meta-learning task setup, GPT-OSS-20B configuration, and path-sampling choices may affect generalization to full agent systems.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Evolving Triple Knowledge-Augmented LLMs for Code Translation in Repository Context
limitacoes: Too many target-language code samples can introduce noise; benchmark target language is Rust only; possible data leakage between RustRepoTrans and target-language sample sources; one-iteration repair is used for cost-effectiveness.
ameacas_validade: Rust-only benchmark, possible overlap between benchmark and retrieved code samples, retrieval strategy settings, translation order randomness, and LLM choice may affect generalizability.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: EXPEREPAIR: Dual-Memory Enhanced LLM-based Repository-Level Program Repair
limitacoes: Less emphasis on bug localization; memories may contain a narrow subset of successful localization cases, limiting diversity and coverage.
ameacas_validade: SWE-bench Lite as sole benchmark, historical repair trajectory selection, LLM choice, cost estimates, and comparison to published baseline numbers may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: DARS has comparable 47.0% resolved rate but nearly six times higher average cost ($12.24 vs $2.07).

title: From Knowledge to Noise: CTIM-Rover and the Pitfalls of Episodic Memory in Software Engineering Agents
limitacoes: Amostra de teste pequena (45 instâncias); ~10% amostras removidas por problemas de ambiente; KD limitado a pares de trajetórias; CTIM não filtrado por relevância ao problema; não investigou retrieval baseado em embeddings como solução para ruído
ameacas_validade: Subset de SWE-bench pode não ser representativo; AutoCodeRover como único framework; distribuição desbalanceada de repositórios (django domina treino e teste); GPT-4o pode não ser ideal para destilação de conhecimento
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Git Context Controller: Manage the Context of LLM-based Agents like Git
limitacoes: Context window fixo de 327K; retrieval window K=1 limita acesso a commits antigos; BRANCH e MERGE aumentam tool calls significativamente; não testado com budget fixo de custo
ameacas_validade: Modelos proprietários de última geração (GPT-5, Claude 4 Sonnet) podem ter capacidades emergentes não generalizáveis; benchmarks artificiais vs produção real; dependência de context windows grandes (327K); ablation apenas com Claude 4 Sonnet
custo_vs_baseline: $1.21/task vs $1.62 SWE-Agent, $2.51 Agentless em SWE-Benchlite (Claude 3.5 S); custo comparável aos melhores open-source apesar de performance muito superior

title: Huxley-Gödel Machine: Human-Level Coding Agent Development by an Approximation of the Optimal Self-Improving Machine
limitacoes: Theoretical result depends on simplifying assumptions; evaluations are costly and partially observed; optimized agents may overfit benchmark-specific behavior; results depend on selected backbones and budgets.
ameacas_validade: SWE-Verified-60 subset, Polyglot setup, local leaderboard comparisons, backbone substitutions, and CPU-hour accounting may affect external validity.
custo_vs_baseline: HGM is reported as 2.38x faster than DGM on SWE-Verified-60 and 6.86x faster than DGM on Polyglot in allocated CPU-hours.

title: Improving Code Localization with Repository Memory
limitacoes: Performance degrada em repositórios com histórico limitado (<10K commits); custo variável e dependente do problema; método de recuperação não otimizado para vocabulário de código; links explícitos entre commits e issues podem ser ruidosos ou ausentes
ameacas_validade: Avaliação apenas em bug fixing/localização; GPT-4o como único modelo; commits pré-issue podem ter qualidade variável; janela fixa de 7K commits pode não ser ideal para todos os repositórios
custo_vs_baseline: Custo maior em problemas difíceis ($0.54→$0.89 quando LocAgent falha); alta variância por exemplo; memória é investimento estratégico em problemas difíceis

title: KernelEvolve: Scaling Agentic Kernel Coding for Heterogeneous AI Accelerators at Meta
limitacoes: Production deployment depends on proprietary hardware, persistent internal knowledge bases, and validation infrastructure; future work is needed to quantify token consumption and optimize prompt design for minimal inference cost.
ameacas_validade: Meta-specific workloads, hardware platforms, proprietary accelerators, production validation stack, and unpublished source code may limit independent replication.
custo_vs_baseline: Development time is reduced from weeks to hours; no per-task API cost is reported.

title: Lingxi: Repository-Level Issue Resolution Framework Enhanced by Procedural Knowledge Guided Scaling
limitacoes: Evaluation centers on SWE-bench Verified; no cost/token reporting captured; method depends on quality and coverage of historical issue-fixing data and LLM-derived procedural knowledge.
ameacas_validade: SWE-bench Verified focus, retrieval/reranking choices, historical issue-pair construction, and proprietary/closed model backbones may affect generalization and replication.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Multi-Agent Code-Orchestrated Generation for Reliable Infrastructure-as-Code
limitacoes: Evaluation is focused on Terraform/IaC generation; no source release or monetary/token cost data captured; memory-curator contribution is positive but smaller than some other component ablations.
ameacas_validade: IaC-Eval benchmark design, synthetic/task distribution, LLM-judge evaluation, Terraform/provider versioning, and multi-agent orchestration choices may affect external validity.
custo_vs_baseline: NR; system includes a Cost and Capacity Planner but no extracted per-task monetary cost is reported.

title: ReasoningBank: Scaling Agent Self-Evolving with Reasoning Memory
limitacoes: Memory extraction and success/failure labeling rely on LLM judgments; software-engineering evidence centers on SWE-bench Verified; monetary cost and detailed memory-size scaling are not extracted here.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark mix, LLM-as-judge reliability, transfer from web to software-engineering tasks, model-family dependence, and reproduction of comparator memory mechanisms may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: Reports efficiency as fewer interaction steps: average 2.8 fewer steps than No Memory and 1.3 fewer than other memory baselines on SWE-bench Verified; no monetary cost extracted.

title: RedCodeAgent: Automatic Red-teaming Agent against Diverse Code Agents
limitacoes: Security red-teaming focus rather than general coding assistance; memory accumulates successful attacks and may depend on scenario sequencing; no monetary/token cost data captured.
ameacas_validade: Sandbox simulation, benchmark scenario selection, target-agent mix, LLM-based planning, dynamic tool selection, and attack-success definitions may affect external validity.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Reflection-Driven Control for Trustworthy Code Agents
limitacoes: Secure-code-generation setting; dynamic memory depends on reflection/verdict quality; qwen3-coder-plus pass rate decreases in the reported summary; source availability is not captured.
ameacas_validade: Scenario construction, vulnerability-class coverage, model selection, verifier quality, LLM reflection reliability, and retrieval fallback thresholds may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: Reports total cost $6.71e-2 for 125 scenarios, average $5.37e-4 per scenario, average $5.67e-4 per successful repair, and API price $1.5e-3 per 1K tokens; no direct baseline monetary comparison extracted.

title: Smarter Together: Creating Agentic Communities of Practice through Shared Experiential Learning
limitacoes: Dados experienciais sintéticos (gerados por GPT-4o, não interações reais); avaliação por LLM-judge sem métricas de execução (Pass@1); DS-1000 como único benchmark (data science); apenas 1 epoch de aprendizado experiencial avaliada
ameacas_validade: LLM-as-judge pode ter correlações com modelos avaliados; experiências sintéticas não representam interações reais; DS-1000 foca data science não SE geral (bug fixing, repository-level); modelos mais fortes beneficiam menos (ceiling effect); Spark é produto comercial, potencial viés
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: SWE-Exp: Experience-Driven Software Issue Resolution
limitacoes: Eficácia depende da qualidade das experiências extraídas; k>1 experiências degradam performance (42.0%→39.0% com k=4); avaliação apenas em projetos Python; Instructor pode se tornar over-dependent de experiências mesmo quando exploração direta é suficiente
ameacas_validade: Modelo único por configuração (DeepSeek ou Claude 4 Sonnet); SWE-bench pode ter overlap com dados de treinamento do LLM; temporal leakage mitigada mas residual (excluem same-repo + post-target); avaliação apenas em Python; avaliação de qualidade da experiência em subset de 75 instâncias
custo_vs_baseline: $0.13 vs $0.12 per issue com DeepSeek (+8.3%); wall time 15min49s vs 12min37s (+25%); retrieval+rerank overhead 37.5s avg

title: TAI3: Testing Agent Integrity in Interpreting User Intent
limitacoes: Focuses on intent-integrity testing rather than production coding assistance; no monetary/token cost data; memory stores mutation strategies rather than software-repair knowledge.
ameacas_validade: Toolkit/API selection, domain coverage, generated task realism, target-model selection, LLM duplicate judgments, and query-budget settings may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: TAI3 reduces AQFF by up to 12% in the UNDERSPEC setting, indicating fewer target-agent queries to expose the first failure; no monetary cost is reported.

title: TALM: Dynamic Tree-Structured Multi-Agent Framework with Long-Term Memory for Scalable Code Generation
limitacoes: Datasets relativamente pequenos (HumanEval 164, ClassEval 100); memória reiniciada a cada round experimental; apenas GPT-4o-mini e GPT-4o testados; apenas geração function/class-level, sem avaliação em repositórios reais (SWE-bench); hyperparameters fixos
ameacas_validade: Apenas modelos OpenAI (GPT-4o-mini, GPT-4o); HumanEval/ClassEval são function/class-level não representativos de SE real; memória reset entre rounds não reflete acumulação contínua; comparação com MapCoder re-implementado pode diferir do original; ClassEval com 100 tasks pode ter alta variância
custo_vs_baseline: ~50% menos tokens totais que MapCoder em ClassEval; overhead marginal de memória vs TALM sem memória

title: TOM-SWE: User Mental Modeling For Software Engineering Agents
limitacoes: User simulator (GPT-5) pode introduzir vieses sistemáticos (over-compliance, over-knowledgeability); 15 perfis derivados de OpenHands sessions podem não representar diversidade global de desenvolvedores; estudo humano observacional sem grupo controle; sessões reais não coletadas por privacidade
ameacas_validade: LLM-as-user-simulator pode não representar comportamento humano real; perfis baseados em desenvolvedores English-speaking usando OpenHands; correlação satisfaction-resolution (r=0.74) baseada em simulator scores; Stateful SWE benchmark novo sem validação externa; single-agent ToM testado limitadamente (N=100)
custo_vs_baseline: $0.17 overhead ToM por sessão (~16% do custo total); GPT-5-nano ToM ($0.02) atinge 38.0% resolve rate no Stateful SWE; custo marginal para ganhos substanciais

title: Toward Generation of Test Cases from Task Descriptions via History-aware Planning
limitacoes: Long-term experience is represented simply as correct/incorrect examples; date pickers, animations, pop-ups, modals, loading screens, and dynamic web elements remain limitations; token use is high.
ameacas_validade: MiniWoB++ and selected real-world web tasks may not generalize to all web applications; baseline replication, task-instance selection, and GPT-4o prompting affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: NR as direct baseline cost; token consumption is reported but not compared against baseline token use.

title: Xolver: Multi-Agent Reasoning with Holistic Experience Learning Just Like an Olympiad Team
limitacoes: Custo computacional elevado (~1.5× tokens de single-agent); dependente da qualidade do backbone LLM; retrieval corpus externo em C++ para tarefas Python (cross-language); self-judging inferior a external judging (-9.9% code, -3.88% math); foco em math e competitive programming, sem avaliação em SE real
ameacas_validade: Benchmarks de math e competitive programming podem não generalizar para SE real; 16-32 runs para variância mas sem confidence intervals formais; retrieval corpus curado (OpenMathReason, cp-algorithms) pode não representar qualidade de retrieval em outros domínios; comparação com modelos proprietários usa resultados reportados oficialmente
custo_vs_baseline: ~1.5× tokens vs Search-o1; complexidade de update O(mI) vs O(n²) do CheatSheet; runtime parallelizável entre agentes

title: AdaExplore: Failure-Driven Adaptation and Diversity-Preserving Search for Efficient Kernel Generation
limitacoes: Kernel-generation focus in Triton and GPU-runtime optimization; skill memory can bias some search strategies toward conservative updates; no cost/token reporting captured.
ameacas_validade: KernelBench task mix, GPU hardware, timing methodology, synthetic task generation, memory filtering thresholds, and model/backbone choices may affect generalization.
custo_vs_baseline: NR; increased compute from 50 to 100 and 200 steps improves speedup, but no monetary or token-cost baseline is extracted.

title: AgentDevel: Reframing Self-Evolving LLM Agents as Release Engineering
limitacoes: Pipeline incurs non-trivial overhead; LLM critic and diagnostic stages can inherit model inconsistencies; may be less suitable for rapid prototyping or highly dynamic environments.
ameacas_validade: Single-agent/single-environment settings, benchmark selection, programmatic scorer availability, release-gate thresholds, and LLM critic reliability may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: Qualitatively higher overhead than single-pass or in-agent refinement due to iterative execution, trace collection, diagnostic scripts, and gating; no exact cost ratio extracted.

title: AgentGA: Evolving Code Solutions in Agent-Seed Space
limitacoes: Only 10 of 16 benchmark runs are reported; long-horizon compute cost is high; seed quality is observed only indirectly through stochastic descendants; parent archives are optional context and can be consumed inconsistently.
ameacas_validade: Partial benchmark coverage, stochastic trajectories, Kaggle task/domain focus, multi-split evaluation choices, tournament operator allocation, and unpublished full artifacts may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: Long-horizon agents consume substantially more tokens and wall-clock time than artifact-centric generation; no direct monetary baseline comparison is reported.

title: APEX-EM: Non-Parametric Online Learning for Autonomous Agents via Structured Procedural-Episodic Experience Replay
limitacoes: Some experiments remain in progress; direct comparisons use different backbones from MemRL; plan compression and learned retrieval policies are future work; remaining MemRL benchmark evaluations are not yet complete.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark/backbone mismatch, LLM judge quality, memory namespace isolation, epoch count, retrieval thresholds, and transfer-split construction may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: Memory reduces average iterations from epoch 1 to epoch 10: BCB reductions of 11-17% and KGQA reductions of 37-43%, but no monetary/token baseline is reported.

title: BEAM: Bi-level Memory-adaptive Algorithmic Evolution for LLM-Powered Heuristic Design
limitacoes: Bi-level structure can require larger initial token consumption; framework may add unnecessary complexity for single-function generation; some comparisons use repository best heuristics with unknown budgets.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark heterogeneity, budget matching, unreproduced baseline results, LLM/model differences, token/time budget settings, and code-execution failures in initial generations may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: BEAM needs more tokens before generating its first heuristic in complex tasks due to the bi-level structure, but Adaptive Memory is intended to reduce repeated code emission; no monetary baseline is reported.

title: Beyond Crash-to-Patch: Patch Evolution for Linux Kernel Repair
limitacoes: End-to-end repair evaluation uses only six recent bugs after strict selection; dataset is scoped to syzbot/Linux kernel workflows; advisor gives diagnostic summaries rather than complete patches.
ameacas_validade: Temporal holdout, syzbot workflow focus, keyword-mapped reviewer categories, small end-to-end bug sample, AI-assessed patch ranking, and compiler/toolchain differences may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Co-RedTeam: Orchestrated Security Discovery and Exploitation with LLM Agents
limitacoes: Security red-team domain focus; memory initialization may affect early performance; multi-agent orchestration depends on execution environments and benchmark-specific validation.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark task mix, curated memory warm start, LLM/model family, iteration budgets, evaluator definitions, and tool/environment realism may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: Latency is lower than OpenHands and C-Agent in reported comparisons, e.g. 198.7s versus OpenHands 219.6s on BountyBench with Gemini-3-pro; no monetary cost baseline reported.

title: Codebase-Memory: Tree-Sitter-Based Knowledge Graphs for LLM Code Exploration via MCP
limitacoes: Graph intentionally omits line-level source context and macro-heavy C performs weakly; dynamic dispatch is limited; benchmark uses one LLM backend and first-author grading.
ameacas_validade: Single LLM backend, manual reference grading, repository/language sample, abstraction limits of Tree-Sitter/graph representation, and single hardware platform for performance benchmarks may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: Reports direct efficiency gains: about 10x fewer tokens, 2.1x fewer tool calls, and query latency under 1 ms versus 10-30 s for file exploration.

title: CoEvoSkills: Self-Evolving Agent Skills via Co-Evolutionary Verification
limitacoes: Ablation rows are single runs due to computational cost; skill evolution depends on verifier quality; results are SkillsBench-centered; generated skills may be domain/model sensitive despite transfer gains.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark composition, deterministic oracle tests, surrogate-verifier information isolation, model/harness choices, number of evolution rounds, and computational budget may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: NR as monetary/token cost; evolution converges by about round 5 and requires verifier/oracle cycles.

title: Complementary Reinforcement Learning
limitacoes: SWE-Bench training uses a curated subset of 124 tasks because many full-dataset tasks are too difficult for smaller models; static experience baselines underperform due to distributional misalignment.
ameacas_validade: Curated task selection, training hyperparameters, Qwen model family, experience extractor size, retrieval/deduplication design, and multi-task contamination may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: Reports behavioral efficiency rather than monetary cost: 1.5x fewer actions on MiniHack Room and 2x fewer actions on ALFWorld; no direct SWE-Bench cost reduction is reported.

title: Controlled Self-Evolution for Algorithmic Code Optimization
limitacoes: Does not yet distill CSE trajectories into model-weight RL training; multi-round evolution still incurs inference cost; results are algorithmic optimization specific.
ameacas_validade: EffiBench-X benchmark composition, candidate budget, fallback to Direct when budget exhausts, model/backbone selection, memory construction order, and embedding retrieval settings may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: NR; reports higher efficiency within the fixed 30-candidate budget but no monetary or token-cost comparison.

title: CORAL: Towards Autonomous Multi-Agent Evolution for Open-Ended Discovery
limitacoes: Relies on frontier foundation models, lacks bootstrapped agent heterogeneity, assumes a reasonably specified evaluator, and multi-agent runs increase API cost.
ameacas_validade: Task-suite composition, hidden or stochastic evaluators, trajectory classification by rule-based and LLM-based analysis, model/runtime choice, and convergence-based stress-test budgets may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: CORAL uses fewer external evaluation calls than structured baselines within the same wall-clock budget, but LLM API calls dominate cost and four-agent runs multiply API cost by about 3-4 times.

title: DARWIN: Dynamic Agentically Rewriting Self-Improving Network
limitacoes: Proof-of-concept on a simple nanoGPT/Shakespeare task, few generations, insignificant results, manual safety checks, and scaling to GPT-2 or larger models would require substantial compute.
ameacas_validade: Small task and model, optimized baseline, only five generations, high mutation probability, simple training code, manual intervention/safety checks, and limited compute constrain validity.
custo_vs_baseline: NR; reports runtime per generation and resource-scaling concerns but no token or monetary comparison.

title: Decocted Experience Improves Test-Time Inference in LLM Agents
limitacoes: Analysis assumes self-experience in experiments, focuses on selected math/web/SWE settings, and leaves more sophisticated closed-loop memory collection and integration for future work.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on base model, embedding model, memory construction from sampled trajectories, K and consolidation hyperparameters, benchmark mix, and plotted rather than fully tabulated task-level values.
custo_vs_baseline: Efficiency is reported through generated output tokens, input-token scaling curves, and interaction steps rather than monetary cost; distilled lessons need fewer input tokens for comparable performance than raw trajectories.

title: DockSmith: Scaling Reliable Coding Environments via an Agentic Docker Builder
limitacoes: Training and evaluation emphasize environment construction; some languages remain bottlenecked by compiler toolchains and system dependencies; context-retrieval errors increase under higher-recall retrieval.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark composition, automated GPT-5.1 error annotation, training-data filtering, token-level mixing ratio, and use of Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-Instruct as base model may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: Compared with Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-Instruct, DockSmith increases average input tokens from 150.10 to 207.68 and output tokens from 13.75 to 26.38 while improving F2P from 19.46% to 39.72%.

title: DomAgent: Leveraging Knowledge Graphs and Case-Based Reasoning for Domain-Specific Code Generation
limitacoes: Future work is needed for AST-based fine-grained similarity, soft prompts, and broader domain adaptation; the truck dataset uses internal documentation and domain-specific infrastructure.
ameacas_validade: Internal truck dataset access, manually curated representative cases and prompts, choice of KG structure, reward model from external LLM, RL training setup, and comparison across different code-generation backbones may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: NR; case selection reduces manual case-base burden by achieving comparable performance with 30% KG-selected cases versus 80% random sampling.

title: EET: Experience-Driven Early Termination for Cost-Efficient Software Engineering Agents
limitacoes: Avaliação limitada a SWE-bench Verified; dependência de dados históricos para cold start em domínios novos; transferibilidade cross-agent não testada; experiências são específicas para cada agente
ameacas_validade: SWE-bench Verified como único benchmark; GPT-5-mini e DeepSeek-V3.2 como únicos modelos; experiências geradas de SWE-bench Lite podem não representar issues reais em produção; threshold tuning no mesmo dataset pode superestimar ganhos
custo_vs_baseline: -31.8% custo total médio vs sem EET; vs Turn-control: -31.8% vs -41.4% (Turn-control reduz mais custo mas degrada resolve rate em -10.7%)

title: EffiSkill: Agent Skill Based Automated Code Efficiency Optimization
limitacoes: Evaluation is on competitive-programming efficiency tasks; real-world software systems may involve libraries, compiler behavior, hardware variation, and cross-file context; broader validation across models, languages, and domains is still needed.
ameacas_validade: Skill quality depends on LLM-generated traces, clustering choices, filtering thresholds, candidate budget, ranking setup, execution environment, and possible distributional similarity between mining and evaluation corpora despite direct-overlap checks.
custo_vs_baseline: All methods use the same k=8 candidate budget; EffiSkill is execution-free during generation and does not rely on online runtime feedback inside the optimization loop.

title: FailureMem: A Failure-Aware Multimodal Framework for Autonomous Software Repair
limitacoes: Detailed memory contexts increase inference cost; performance depends on diversity and coverage of the offline failure-memory bank; rare or unprecedented failures may lack similar retrieved memories; automated repair still requires regression checks and human oversight.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on SWE-bench Multimodal, model choice, memory-bank construction from development failures, retrieval depth, visual-grounding tools, and GPU resources; larger or different GUI repositories may change memory usefulness.
custo_vs_baseline: FailureMem reports higher inference cost than GUIRepair, USD 0.33 versus USD 0.29 per issue, while adding active perception and memory guidance.

title: Group-Evolving Agents: Open-Ended Self-Improvement via Experience Sharing
limitacoes: Evaluation is expensive and relies on staged benchmark subsets; results depend on model choice, group size, parent-selection settings, and benchmark-specific evolution dynamics.
ameacas_validade: Staged selection from small sanity and mini-benchmark subsets may influence which agents reach full evaluation; benchmark scores depend on tool environments and large model APIs; human-designed framework comparisons are not identical training/evolution protocols.
custo_vs_baseline: GEA and the DGM-style baseline use the same number of evolved agents, so evaluation cost is reported as similar under the staged benchmark protocol.

title: KAPSO: A Knowledge-grounded framework for Autonomous Program Synthesis and Optimization
limitacoes: Número limitado de competições no ALE-Bench introduz ruído; variância entre tarefas; dependência de LLMs proprietários (Gemini); custo elevado por tarefa; knowledge package pode ter viés de seleção
ameacas_validade: MLE-Bench e ALE-Bench são competições ML/algoritmos, não SE tradicional (bug fixing, code localization); Gemini-2.5-pro como único modelo; knowledge base construída pelos autores com critérios de seleção potencialmente subjetivos
custo_vs_baseline: ALE-Bench: $914.8 vs $1003.3 ALE-Agent (-8.8%); MLE-Bench: orçamento fixo de $200 não permite comparação direta

title: KernelBlaster: Continual Cross-Task CUDA Optimization via Memory-Augmented In-Context Reinforcement Learning
limitacoes: Avaliação limitada a kernels CUDA NVIDIA; Level 3 tem scaling challenges com single optimization por iteração; Knowledge Base pode ter viés para entradas iniciais; dependência de NCU profiling detalhado para feedback de qualidade
ameacas_validade: KernelBench como único benchmark; GPUs NVIDIA específicas (Ampere, Hopper, Ada); reward hacking possível mesmo com verificação de corretude; comparação com AI CUDA Engineer sob condições de avaliação diferentes (modelos, hardware, test harness)
custo_vs_baseline: 2.4× menos tokens que minimal agent sem KB; KB reutilizável entre GPUs (~50KB compacta)

title: KernelSkill: A Multi-Agent Framework for GPU Kernel Optimization
limitacoes: Some baselines are not open-sourced, so several comparisons use reported results or reimplementations rather than a single identical codebase; evaluation is limited to KernelBench GPU-kernel tasks.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on NVIDIA A100 hardware, profiling tools, seed selection, fixed refinement rounds, threshold settings, ChatGPT-5.1 behavior, and comparability of non-open-source baseline results.
custo_vs_baseline: KernelSkill reports stronger speedup per refinement round than STARK: 0.36/0.19/0.13 on Levels 1/2/3 versus 0.10/0.09/0.05 for STARK.

title: Large Language Model-Powered Evolutionary Code Optimization on a Phylogenetic Tree
limitacoes: The system lacks hardware-aware cost models, can be inefficient for computationally heavy workloads, focuses on NVIDIA GPUs, does not address multi-GPU or distributed execution, and may produce occasional low-quality LLM-generated modifications.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on empirical runtime measurement, hardware/backend choice, benchmark workload selection, container execution, pruning settings, LLM proposal quality, and the absence of a theoretical account of when trajectory reuse yields stable improvement operators.
custo_vs_baseline: No monetary or token-cost comparison is reported; the discussion emphasizes that trajectory-conditioned reuse reduces redundant exploration but still depends on empirical evaluation.

title: LatentMem: Customizing Latent Memory for Multi-Agent Systems
limitacoes: The paper emphasizes effectiveness and efficiency but still depends on collected in-domain trajectories, learned composer behavior, role-profile quality, and benchmark/MAS framework coverage; out-of-domain generalization is evaluated but not exhaustive.
ameacas_validade: Results may depend on synthetic coding and planning benchmarks, selected MAS frameworks, trajectory collection protocol, composer base model, LMPO reward design, latent-length and retrieval-count settings, and LLM-as-component implementation choices.
custo_vs_baseline: LatentMem is reported as fastest and lowest-token among memory baselines in the added cost figures; examples include 1.76e6 tokens on AutoGen+KodCode versus G-Memory at 3.04e6 and 1.09e4 seconds on MacNet+KodCode versus G-Memory at 2.02e4.

title: Learning to Commit: Generating Organic Pull Requests via Online Repository Memory
limitacoes: Evaluation is a pilot on one highly coupled expert-maintained repository with 7 future held-out tasks; LLM judges may introduce bias; public multi-repository generalization is left for future work.
ameacas_validade: Synthetic issue generation, LLM-based commit filtering and judging, small future-test sample, single repository domain, temporal split construction, and skill verbosity may influence the organicity results.
custo_vs_baseline: The skill-conditioned agent uses fewer trajectory steps in seq-all (56.8 versus 71.9) and slightly fewer or similar steps in most settings, but no monetary cost is reported.

title: MEMCoder: Multi-dimensional Evolving Memory for Private-Library-Oriented Code Generation
limitacoes: Additional memory injection and reflection introduce token overhead; long-term memory growth may cause retrieval inefficiency and knowledge conflicts; benchmark scope is private-library generation on NdonnxEval and NumbaEval.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on local open-source models, bge-base-en-prior.5 retrieval, task ordering, memory-guideline quality, execution feedback reliability, private-library benchmark representativeness, and memory conflict management.
custo_vs_baseline: MEMCoder adds token overhead for memory injection and guideline refinement compared with naive RAG, but the paper estimates the added full-NumbEval cost as about USD 0.46.

title: MemCollab: Cross-Agent Memory Collaboration via Contrastive Trajectory Distillation
limitacoes: Direct memory transfer between models can degrade performance; too many retrieved memory entries can reduce performance after a task-dependent threshold; evaluation spans math and code benchmarks rather than full software-engineering workflows.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on task classification, TF-IDF retrieval, top-3 memory budget, paired-agent choice, benchmark sample construction, random seed, and whether contrastive summaries suppress model-specific biases without dropping useful details.
custo_vs_baseline: MemCollab reduces average reasoning turns for Qwen2.5-7B: MATH500 2.7 to 2.2, GSM8K 1.8 to 1.6, MBPP 3.1 to 1.4, and HumanEval 3.3 to 1.5.

title: MemGovern: Enhancing Code Agents through Learning from Governed Human Experiences
limitacoes: Overhead de tokens adicionais para busca na memória experiencial; experience cards construídos offline via GPT-5.1 (custo de construção não reportado); estratégias de compressão de memória não exploradas; Index Layer remove especificidade de repositório
ameacas_validade: SWE-bench Verified como único benchmark; experience cards geradas por GPT-5.1 podem ter viés; 150K triplets GitHub pré-filtrados podem sobrepor temporalmente com SWE-bench; avaliação de quality control via LLM-judge; human evaluation limitada a 100 repositórios para seleção
custo_vs_baseline: Overhead moderado: $6.94→$7.27 com Claude 4 Sonnet (+4.8%); $0.14→$0.20 com GPT-4o-Mini (+43%); proporcional ao uso de Searching/Browsing tools

title: Memory Transfer Learning: How Memories are Transferred Across Domains in Coding Agents
limitacoes: Memory transfer can cause negative transfer through domain-mismatched anchors, false validation confidence, and misapplied best-practice transfer; advanced static retrieval strategies underperform simple embedding similarity.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on sampled benchmark tasks, GPT-5-mini memory generation and judging, embedding retrieval, source-domain composition, memory format prompts, cross-model transfer setup, and whether benchmark domains represent real deployment diversity.
custo_vs_baseline: MTL uses fewer stored memories than AgentKB in the self-evolving comparison (431 versus 5,899) while reaching a higher average score; no monetary or token-cost comparison is reported.

title: ODEL: An Experience-Augmented Self-Evolving Framework for Efficient Python-to-C++ Code Translation
limitacoes: The multi-phase evaluation uses a fixed HumanEval-X task set and consistent task ordering; results reflect sustained learning under a stable stream rather than strict order-invariant generalization.
ameacas_validade: Findings depend on generated test cases, verification feedback quality, HumanEval-X representativeness, external teacher model quality, task ordering, and potential leakage concerns mitigated by storing abstract error patterns rather than task-specific solutions.
custo_vs_baseline: No numeric cost comparison is reported; ODEL invokes the external DeepSeek 685B teacher only on verification failure, while routine translation remains with the internal model.

title: OpenGame: Open Agentic Coding for Games
limitacoes: Even the full OpenGame leaves about 34.9% of weighted mechanical requirements partially or fully unsatisfied; strategy and Puzzle/UI genres remain difficult because silent logic desynchronization may not trigger compiler or runtime errors.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on OpenGame-Bench prompt design, VLM judging, Phaser instruction constraints for baselines, valid-run filtering, browser execution, seed averaging, and whether web-game tasks generalize to other software domains or engines.
custo_vs_baseline: No numeric cost comparison is reported; ablation shows bounded iterative debugging improves reliability and begins to plateau after three iterations.

title: OpenSage: Self-programming Agent Generation Engine
limitacoes: The paper notes that current SOTA models do not yet use OpenSage's advanced features consistently and may misuse them, indicating a model-capability gap for AI-centered agent construction.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on benchmark leaderboards, selected backbones, added task-specific code for SWE-Bench Pro, graph-memory schema/tool design, generated node types, memory-agent decisions, and whether ADK-level gains generalize beyond the evaluated environments.
custo_vs_baseline: The Terminal-Bench collaboration analysis reports that pairing Gemini 3 Pro for planning/review with GPT-5 Mini for execution matches GPT-5 performance while reducing cost compared with Gemini 3 Pro alone.

title: Outcome-Conditioned Reasoning Distillation for Resolving Software Issues
limitacoes: The evaluation is limited to SWE-Bench Lite and depends on same-repository historical fixes; exemplar mismatch can cause negative transfer, and the cost comparison is against a controlled MCTS-style baseline rather than every possible search system.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on temporal filtering of historical issues, quality of verified patches, Agentless integration choices, LLM compatibility judgments, SWE-Bench Lite representativeness, and whether similar historical repairs exist for future repositories.
custo_vs_baseline: In the reasoning-plan ablation, O-CRD with BRD achieves higher Pass@1 than the MCTS-style variant while using substantially fewer LLM calls and tokens.

title: Procedural Knowledge at Scale Improves Reasoning
limitacoes: Document retrieval can degrade reasoning models; the method can fail when self-verbalized queries become over-specific or when retrieved procedures mismatch the problem setup; benefits vary after similar corpus knowledge is internalized by model training.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on Nemotron-derived trajectory coverage, decomposition prompt quality, ReasonIR retrieval, benchmark contamination assumptions, sample-budget choices, and whether math/science/coding reasoning transfers to agentic software-engineering workflows.
custo_vs_baseline: The method can outperform retrieval-free Length Scaling at matched sample budgets, but the paper does not provide monetary or end-to-end token-cost comparisons.

title: ReCreate: Reasoning and Creating Domain Agents Driven by Experience
limitacoes: ReCreate optimizes textual and code-level scaffolds but not infrastructure or execution environments, and it does not update base model parameters.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on small sampled development sets, benchmark-specific scaffold design spaces, use of gpt-5-mini and claude-4.5-opus, Docker sandbox execution, cost-estimation assumptions, and whether domain-agent creation from minimal seeds generalizes beyond the tested domains.
custo_vs_baseline: Compared with ADAS, ReCreate is reported to reduce scaffold-optimization cost by roughly 36% to 82% because it uses interaction evidence and smaller development sets rather than repeated candidate evaluation.

title: REVERE: Reflective Evolving Research Engineer for Scientific Workflows
limitacoes: Prompt and Global Training Context growth remain a challenge; REVERE still shows degraded CodeBERTScore on ScienceAgentBench relative to Static SOTA, and the paper notes the difficulty of capturing highly task-specific knowledge through prompt benchmarks.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on GPT-4.1's long context window, benchmark split choices, use of optional ground-truth hints in offline adaptation, estimated cost accounting, and whether research-coding heuristics transfer to broader software-engineering tasks.
custo_vs_baseline: REVERE is reported to be up to about 10x more cost-effective than ACE and GEPA in adaptation overhead, using targeted edits and Global Training Context rather than refinement and Pareto selection.

title: RuleFlow: Generating Reusable Program Optimizations with LLMs
limitacoes: LLM-generated rules can be syntactically invalid, over-fitted, semantically divergent with null values, or unsafe under data-flow dependencies; the paper does not provide formal correctness guarantees for rewrite rules.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on PandasBench representativeness, default unscaled notebook workloads, qualitative manual rule filtering, runtime preconditions, Pandas data-corner cases, and whether reusable rewrite rules generalize beyond Pandas optimization.
custo_vs_baseline: RuleFlow avoids per-program LLM calls at deployment by amortizing offline LLM discovery into reusable compiler rules, but it does not report monetary or token costs for discovery.

title: Scaling Teams or Scaling Time? Memory Enabled Lifelong Learning in LLM Multi-Agent Systems
limitacoes: The Qwen3-next-80B research setting shows reduced AAS despite higher task score, larger teams can incur coordination overhead and non-monotonic performance, and the study does not directly measure memory redundancy, staleness, or retrieval robustness.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on MultiAgentBench collaborative tasks, Claude-Sonnet-4.5 evaluation scoring, Amazon Bedrock inference settings, Titan embedding retrieval, chosen consolidation intervals, and whether coding/research/database environments represent broader multi-agent software work.
custo_vs_baseline: LLMA-Mem lowers average token usage relative to MARBLE and A-Mem while improving long-horizon performance, showing that gains are not obtained by simply spending more context tokens.

title: SEVerA: Verified Self-Evolving Agents
limitacoes: Resource usage is not part of the formal constraints; the framework does not account for LLM calls, token budgets, or wall-clock budgets in synthesized agents.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on formal specification quality, verifier coverage, task selection, chosen LLM backbones, bounded candidate budget, and the representativeness of constrained verification and tau2-bench tasks.
custo_vs_baseline: Full SEVerA pipeline takes 18.2 seconds per HumanEvalDafny instance versus 9.8 seconds for an LLM baseline, and 25.6 seconds versus 10.3 seconds on DafnyBench.

title: Software Self-Extension with SelfEvolve: an Agentic Architecture for Runtime Code Generation
limitacoes: Evaluation uses only 11 manually designed tasks, one LLM/function-calling stack, and functional-correctness metrics; it does not establish regression guarantees for old functions or broader performance/security/maintainability properties.
ameacas_validade: Potential test-code collusion because the same LLM generates tests and code, small benchmark size, manually created tasks, OpenAI-specific function-calling interface, and limited evidence for multi-function or long-running deployment settings.
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Structurally Aligned Subtask-Level Memory for Software Engineering Agents
limitacoes: Avaliação apenas no SWE-bench Verified; memória reiniciada a cada round experimental (não testa acumulação contínua real); extração de experiências pelo mesmo LLM backbone pode limitar qualidade; top-1 retrieval pode perder experiências relevantes
ameacas_validade: SWE-bench Verified predominantemente Python; memória reiniciada prejudica avaliação de escalabilidade long-term; 4 modelos testados mas apenas famílias Google e Anthropic; Instance-level Memory é reprodução de ReasoningBank, não implementação original; variância entre runs (σ até 2.27)
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: SuperLocalMemory V3.3: The Living Brain -- Biologically-Inspired Forgetting, Cognitive Quantization, and Multi-Channel Retrieval for Zero-LLM Agent Memory Systems
limitacoes: Fusion complexity hurts simple/single-hop retrieval, fixed channel weights create cold-start sensitivity, and zero-friction lifecycle hooks are Claude Code-specific.
ameacas_validade: Single-system authorship, local benchmark design, LoCoMo mode choices, retrieval-channel weights, platform-specific hooks, and comparison to the prior V3.2 system may limit generalization.
custo_vs_baseline: Zero-LLM Mode A avoids LLM/API cost and runs on local CPU resources, but the paper does not isolate a comparable monetary-cost baseline.

title: TextBFGS: A Case-Based Reasoning Approach to Code Optimization via Error-Operator Retrieval
limitacoes: Evaluation focuses on filtered coding-optimization benchmarks and case trajectories from HumanEval/MBPP; generalization to larger repositories and richer optimization objectives is not established.
ameacas_validade: Filtered hard-task construction, case-base source domain, retrieval embedding design, baseline reimplementation choices, and maximum-iteration limits may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: Compared with TextGrad-Momentum on HumanEval-Hard, TextBFGS uses about 50.6% fewer tokens per task; the paper also reports lower calls per task than TextGrad/TextGrad-Momentum.

title: Toward Autonomous Long-Horizon Engineering for ML Research
limitacoes: Evaluation is expensive and constrained to benchmarked ML research engineering tasks; performance depends on specialized agent roles, workspace conventions, and available compute/time budgets.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark selection, 24-hour task limit, H20 GPU environment, grading model, selected backbones, workspace permissions, and comparison to simplified agents may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: AiScientist is cheaper than IterAgent in the reported PaperBench setup: $15.67 versus $27.44 per task with Gemini and $12.20 versus $54.90 with GLM-5.

title: Traversal-as-Policy: Log-Distilled Gated Behavior Trees as Externalized, Verifiable Policies for Safe, Robust, and Efficient Agents
limitacoes: Coverage boundary explícito: fora de coverage (covered=0), GBT só garante primitive-level safety; distillation de logs pode herdar vieses dos trajectórios OpenHands; 21 task families fixas podem não cobrir todos domínios; gates dependem de structured ctx que pode omitir estado relevante
ameacas_validade: GPT-4o como base model primário; GBT distilado de logs OpenHands pode não transferir para outros frameworks; safety gates determinísticos sobre ctx podem falhar se ctx omitir campos relevantes; avaliação Protocol A (cross-benchmark distillation) pode ter overlap semântico residual; macro abstraction stability testada mas não garantida para todos cenários
custo_vs_baseline: Tok/Chars 208k/820k→126k/490k SWE-bench (-39%/-40%); 94k/360k→52k/205k WebArena (-45%/-43%); 8B executors viáveis com same GBT artifact

title: When the Specification Emerges: Benchmarking Faithfulness Loss in Long-Horizon Coding Agents
limitacoes: ProjectGuard is evaluated as a mitigation case study for emergent specification, not as a complete standalone coding-agent architecture across many software-engineering domains.
ameacas_validade: Component scoring rubric, paper selection, emergent request scripts, Claude Code/Codex platform behavior, hidden artifact design, and ProjectGuard implementation choices may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: ProjectGuard adds external state averaging hundreds of KB and changes compaction/restart behavior; no monetary or token-cost comparison is reported.

title: Your Code Agent Can Grow Alongside You with Structured Memory
limitacoes: Avaliação apenas no SWE-bench Verified; depende de histórico de commits rico; qualidade da memória limitada pela capacidade do LLM de destilação; overhead de retrieval não quantificado; ablação em subset de 200 instâncias
ameacas_validade: SWE-bench Verified como único benchmark; DeepSeek-V3.2 e GPT-5.2 como únicos modelos; memória construída de commits do mesmo repositório avaliado (temporal leakage controlada mas residual); cross-encoder reranker pode introduzir latência não reportada
custo_vs_baseline: NR

title: Self-Adapting Agents for Automating Research Coding Workflows
limitacoes: Reflective calls and growing Global Training Context introduce overhead and can approach context-window limits; evaluation is concentrated on research-coding benchmarks and selected baselines.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark representativeness, train/validation/test split choices, use of ground-truth traces in offline adaptation, single LLM stack, anonymous artifact status, and comparison-baseline implementation choices may affect validity.
custo_vs_baseline: Per-task benchmark costs are reported at roughly $1-3, but the paper does not isolate the additional cost of reflection and context growth versus baselines.
